rationale: This page contains detailed comparative information on registered vs. recognized political parties, the legal framework for Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls, and the appointment/removal process for the Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) under the 2023 Act.
cleanedText:
However, as per Guidelines for registration of Political Parties, parties not contesting elections for 6 consecutive years can be removed from the register. ### Differences between Registered and Recognized Political Parties | Aspects | Registered Political Parties | Recognized Political Parties | | :--- | :--- | :--- | | Recognition Status | Registered with ECI (Section 29A of RPA, 1951) | Recognition as State or National Party | | Election Symbol | Preference in matter of allotment of election symbols | Use reserved symbol during general election | | Election Perks | • 10 proposers are needed.
• Common symbol for contesting general elections to Lok Sabha/State Assemblies
• Maximum of 20 Star Campaigners | • Need only one proposer for filing nomination.
• Broadcast/telecast facilities over Akashvani/Doordarshan during general election
• Maximum of 40 Star Campaigners | ## 5.2. SPECIAL INTENSIVE REVISION **Why in the news?** Recently, ECI has published the draft electoral rolls following the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) in West Bengal, Rajasthan, Goa, Lakshadweep and Puducherry. **About Special Intensive Revision** * It is legally backed by Article 324 of Constitution and Section 21(3) of RPA, 1950. * It is a House-to-House verification done by Booth Level Officers to ensure enrolment of all eligible Citizens in Electoral Roll. * SIR was conducted eight times from 1951 till 2004, with last exercise undertaken over 21 years ago. **Electoral Rolls** * Electoral roll is a list of persons registered as electors residing in a constituency. * Article 326 of the Constitution, and Section 19 of Representation of the People Act, 1950 stipulate that the minimum age for registration of a voter as 18 years. * Through the 61st Amendment Act, 1988 the minimum age of registration of a voter was brought down to 18 years from 21. ## 5.3. OTHER IMPORTANT NEWS ### 5.3.1. APPOINTMENT AND REMOVAL OF CHIEF ELECTION COMMISSIONER (CEC) CEC, ECs and their Appointment is done under Chief Election Commissioner and Other Election Commissioner Act, 2023. * **The 2023 Act provides more autonomy to the ECI as under the Act:** * CEC and ECs enjoy Supreme Court judge-level salary & benefits. * Granted immunity from civil/criminal proceedings for official duties. * Search & selection committees for appointments. * Fixed tenure for CEC & ECs. **Process for Removal (as mentioned under Article 124(4))** * Article 324(5) states that CEC can be removed from office only "in like manner and on like grounds as a Judge of the Supreme Court." * Thus, removal of CEC begins with a motion in either House alleging misbehaviour or incapacity. * Once the motion is admitted, a committee enquires into validity of the charges.
